summary: Code-quality PR across fighter, game-mode, stage, item, and menu files to satisfy local `melee-issues`: mostly prototype cleanup for no-argument functions, removal of unused or duplicate declarations/includes, callback table formatting/cast cleanup, and broad formatting normalization. The PR body is empty and there are no review comments; the only loaded issue comment is a bot report saying `No changes` for GALE01.

key_files.role: Removed an unused mp/mplib include and converted K&R-style fn_800DBED4 parameter declaration to a normal typed prototype.
Formatting-only brace split in checkNanaInRange condition.
Formatting normalization for casts, condition wrapping, and helper-call argument layout in dynamic bone code.
Changed empty parameter list to explicit void for fn_80171DC4.
Changed fn_80188644 to explicit void and collapsed fn_8018846C_noInline forward declaration plus definition into a static function definition.
Changed inline no-argument helpers fn_801A7FB4_inline and fn_801A7FB4_inline2 to explicit void parameter lists.
Changed static inline no-argument helper isActiveTitle to explicit void parameter list.
Formatting normalization in StageCallbacks initializer and pointer-cast spacing in route selection code.
Changed no-argument functions grCastle_801D0BBC and grCastle_801D0D24 to explicit void parameter lists.
Reordered itwhispyapple include with other item includes and normalized long-line formatting across Greens stage logic.
Removed an extra blank line in declarations.
Changed inline no-argument random helper to explicit void parameter list.
Moved early Zebes function definitions below data declarations, removed local forward declarations and callback casts/compound flag braces, and reformatted callback initializers.
Changed static no-argument ordering_func to explicit void parameter list.
Changed inline no-argument GetNumNameList to explicit void parameter list.

what_changed: Converted many no-argument C functions from empty parameter lists to explicit `(void)`, including game-mode, stage, item, and menu helpers.
Replaced a K&R-style function definition for `fn_800DBED4` with a normal typed parameter declaration.
Removed an unused include from `ftCo_Attack100.c` and reordered an item include in `grgreens.c`.
Changed `fn_8018846C_noInline` from a separate non-static prototype plus definition to a single `static int fn_8018846C_noInline(void)` definition.
In `grzebes.c`, moved several function definitions after data declarations and simplified `StageCallbacks` entries by removing explicit function-pointer casts and `{ 0 }` flag initializers.
Performed broad formatting cleanup: cast spacing, brace placement, long-line wrapping, callback table wrapping, and removal of an extra blank line.
Loaded bot issue comment reported `No changes` for GALE01; no human review comments were present in the slice.

smart_moves.text: The PR limits itself to lint/code-quality surface changes rather than mixing in decomp rewrites or semantic renames.
Converting empty no-argument lists to `(void)` makes function signatures explicit without changing symbol names or bodies.
Removing local clutter such as an unused include, an extra blank line, and a redundant forward declaration keeps the patch focused on issue cleanup.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Local lint-fix PRs can safely improve reviewability by making no-argument functions explicit with `(void)` while preserving address-style or unknown-meaning symbol names.
When a helper is only needed in its translation unit, replacing a separate declaration plus definition with a `static` definition can reduce exposed surface area.
Callback table cleanup should be kept mechanical: remove unnecessary casts only where function signatures already match the table shape, and avoid semantic changes in the same patch.

naming_conventions.convention: The patch keeps existing address-style and subsystem-style names such as `fn_80171DC4`, `grZebes_801D84A4`, and `GetNumNameList`; it does not introduce speculative semantic renames.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: No assembly-specific matching tactic is evidenced. Existing `PAD_STACK(4)` in `fn_800DBED4` remains unchanged; this PR only changes that function's declaration style.
The available bot comment says `No changes` for GALE01, but the slice does not include detailed objdiff/checkdiff output or per-symbol validation.

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments are present in the loaded PR slice.
Automated issue comment from `decomp-dev[bot]` reported `No changes` for GALE01.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `melee-issues` or local lint rules for explicit `(void)` function signatures.
Search for remaining empty no-argument definitions in `src/melee` after PR 2465.
Search for callback tables still using unnecessary function-pointer casts or `{ 0 }` flag initializers.
Search for K&R-style function definitions in decomp source, especially address-style `fn_` functions.
Check whether `decomp-dev[bot] No changes` comments are used as accepted regression evidence in other PR intakes.

PR body is empty, so intent is inferred mainly from title, label, and diff shape.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use explicit `(void)` for no-argument C functions in lint cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2465 repeatedly converts no-argument functions from `func()` to `func(void)` across gm, gr, it, and mn files as part of local `melee-issues` cleanup.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Avoid speculative renames during code-quality cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR fixes signatures, includes, casts, and formatting while preserving existing address-style names, keeping lint cleanup separate from semantic naming work.

source_update_candidates.title: Document explicit `(void)` no-argument function style for C lint cleanup
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a source-owned code-quality convention that C functions taking no parameters should be declared/defined with `(void)`, especially when touching local `melee-issues` fixes.
source_update_candidates.reason: This pattern is repeated across many files in the PR, but the intake slice does not prove it is already an accepted global standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer narrow mechanical lint PRs over mixed semantic decomp edits
source_update_candidates.text: Consider documenting that local issue/lint cleanup should stay mechanical: prototypes, includes, declarations, casts, and formatting, without speculative renames or matching rewrites.
source_update_candidates.reason: PR 2465 is a useful example of narrow lint cleanup, but broader policy belongs in curated standards.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every callback-table cast can be removed globally; the PR only shows this in `grzebes.c` where the edited entries apparently match expected signatures.
Do not treat the bot `No changes` comment as detailed per-symbol objdiff evidence; no detailed validation log is loaded.
Do not promote any claim about why functions were moved in `grzebes.c` beyond the observable diff and title.
